text.title: Freelancer Personal CRM Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every solo-freelancer CRM application must enforce. These are the HARD rules; team-CRM features must not override them.

rule.statement: Use exactly three tables: Leads, Clients, Invoices. Reject any schema with more than three top-level tables for solo use.
rule.rationale: Solo operators abandon CRMs that ask for > 3 tables within 14 days. Three is the cognitive ceiling for weekly maintenance without rituals decaying.

rule.statement: Each table has at most five mandatory fields. Leads: name, source, stage, next_action_date, value_estimate. Clients: name, start_date, hourly_or_fixed, retainer_end_date, health. Invoices: client, amount, issued_date, due_date, status.
rule.rationale: Field count past 5 correlates with first-week neglect > 60%. Optional fields are added later only when a real workflow demands them.

rule.statement: Lead stages are a closed enum: new → qualified → proposal_sent → negotiating → won OR lost. Transitions require an explicit dated note. No "kinda qualified" or freeform stages.
rule.rationale: Freeform stages let 30-40% of leads die in undefined states. Closed enum forces a decision per touch.

rule.statement: Default cadence after each cold outreach: 3 days, 7 days, 14 days, 30 days. After day 30 with no reply: mark lost. Operator may extend only after dated proof of "asked me to wait."
rule.rationale: Reply probability past day 30 without a wait-signal drops below 4%. Continuing past that wastes capacity and pollutes the pipeline.

rule.statement: Any invoice unpaid 30 days past due_date triggers a hard alarm in the weekly review. At 45 days, the operator must either send a final-notice email or pause work on that client. No silent aging beyond 45 days.
rule.rationale: Probability of collecting an invoice drops from ~95% at day 30 to < 50% at day 90. Day 30 is the cheapest intervention point.

example.title: Minimal Leads row
example.code: name: Acme Corp / Jane Doe source: LinkedIn DM 2026-05-10 stage: proposal_sent next_action_date: 2026-05-22 value_estimate: $4,800 (6 weeks × $200/hr × 4 hrs/day)
example.note: Illustrates r2 and r3: 5 fields, closed-enum stage, dated next action.

example.title: Bloated schema that gets abandoned
example.code:
Fields: name, company, role, email, phone, linkedin, twitter, website, budget_min, budget_max, ICP_score, lead_score, BANT_authority, BANT_need, BANT_timing, custom_field_1, custom_field_2, ...
Result: 18 fields. Operator stops updating after 9 days. CRM rot.
example.note: Illustrates r2 violation. Solo CRMs that look like Salesforce die from maintenance debt.

example.title: Invoice aging alarm
example.code: issued_date: 2026-04-01 due_date: 2026-04-15 today: 2026-05-20 days_overdue: 35 → ALARM (rule r5, past day 30) action: send final-notice email by Friday OR pause work
example.note: Illustrates r5: day-30 alarm forces decision before collection probability collapses.
